effect.name: Fast Lookahead Limiter
effect.description:
Fast Lookahead Limiter (LADSPA 1913)
This is a limiter with an attack time of 5ms. It adds just over 5ms of latency to the input signal, but it guarantees that there will be no signals over the limit, and tries to get the minimum amount of distortion.
effect.author: Steve Harris (http://www.ladspa.org)

parameter.name: Input Gain
parameter.comment:
Gain that is applied
to the input stage. Can be used to trim gain to bring it roughly under the limit or to push the signal against the limit.

parameter.name: Limit
parameter.comment:
The maximum output amplitude.
Peaks over this level will be attenuated as smoothly as possible to bring them as close as possible to this level.

parameter.name: Release Time
parameter.comment:
The time taken for the
limiters attenuation to return to 0 dB

parameter.name: Attenuation
parameter.comment:
The current attenuation
of the signal coming out of the delay buffer
